title: Cuts and puncture wounds
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: A laceration is a break or opening in the skin and may require stitches.
Deep wounds can involve tendons, nerves, blood vessels, muscles, ligaments, or bone.
Infection warning signs include warmth, redness, pain, fever, swelling, red streaking, or pus-like drainage.
Bites, punctures, dirty wounds, crush injuries, and foot wounds have higher infection risk.
